_doc: Tunable comp-selection criteria. Edit this file to retune WITHOUT touching code. Jurisdiction logic lives in src/screener/plugins/<jurisdiction>.py; this file only carries parameters. Every value here traces to a dated entry in DECISIONS.md.
_activated_products_doc: Building-class first letters ACTIVATED in v1. Only Office ('O') is live (DECISIONS 2026-06-19). A subject whose class letter is not here gets an 'out of scope for v1' refusal. Adding a type later (e.g. 'K' retail) is a config addition here plus its bucket map, not an engine change.
_office_buckets_doc: Office subclass -> bucket key (DECISIONS 2026-06-19). O1-O4 each their own bucket (exact match, no cross-matching); O5+O6 grouped; O7+O8+O9 grouped. Class code encodes stories at 100% fill, so common codes stay exact for tight peers and rare codes group to avoid comp starvation. A comp must share the subject's bucket.
_fallback_ladder_doc: Tiered class fallback for the EXACT low-rise codes (DECISIONS 2026-06-20). Distance is relaxed BEFORE class: a subject's exact class is exhausted across the full radius (0.5->1.0 mi) first; only if it yields < min_comp_count at 1.0 mi are adjacent classes added, in ladder order, each swept 0.5->1.0 mi. O4 has an empty ladder = exact-or-refuse (towers have no adjacent peer class). Classes NOT listed here (O5/O6, O7/O8/O9) are grouped buckets that 'fall back within the bucket only': the whole bucket is the exact match unit, with no cross-bucket ladder. The grouped bucket map (office_buckets) is unchanged.
_sf_band_doc: Gross-building-area band: a comp must fall within +/- this fraction of the subject's gross building area. LOCKED 0.50 (DECISIONS 2026-06-19), tunable in Phase 6.
_sf_required_doc: Only parcels with a reported gross building area enter the comp set, and the subject must have one too (gross SF is part of the +/-50% match).
_location_mode_doc: Comps ranked/collected by great-circle distance from the subject's PLUTO latitude/longitude (DECISIONS 2026-06-19). ZIP is NOT a match criterion.
_radius_doc: Radius-first with automatic expansion (DECISIONS 2026-06-19; start lowered 0.5->0.25 citywide 2026-06-29). Named ladder (start, step, cap) so a future metro can set its own. Collect ALL qualifying comps within radius_start_miles; if fewer than min_comp_count, expand by radius_step_miles toward radius_cap_miles; if still short at the cap, REFUSE. The actual radius used is reported on every result. This band governs the AUTO ladder ONLY — the manual OVERRIDE slider keeps its own [0.1, 2.0] range and fixes the radius with no auto-expansion. Radii tunable.
_zip_prefilter_doc: Optional ZIP pre-filter, OFF by default. When true, candidates are additionally restricted to the subject's ZIP before distance ranking. Demoted from a match criterion to an optional pre-filter only.
_retail_classification_doc: Retail (K) parcels are classified by MEASURED PLUTO floor-area share, not by K-code (diagnosis: PLUTO retailarea is 98% filled, ~99% reconciled). retail_share = retailarea/bldgarea. >= retail_share_pure_threshold -> pure_retail (per-SF shown); below -> mixed, with second use by mixed_use_second_use_threshold (office precedence over residential). Per-SF gating is by retail_share ALONE, independent of category. Tunable per-metro. Stage 1 = classifier module only; live retail screening is later stages.

industrial_config.scope_rule: bldg_class starts with 'F' (ALL F-subcodes in scope, incl. F8)

_industrial_config_doc: Industrial (F-code) comp parameters, LOCKED 2026-07-01 (DECISIONS.md 'Industrial F-code comp configuration'). LIVE on public /screen: F-codes are intercepted in _screen_view and routed to build_industrial_screen_view (same K-only pattern retail uses; the broad out_of_scope gate is untouched). activated_products stays ['O'] — like retail K, industrial goes live via the interception, not this list. All numbers here are tunable config, not hardcoded. SCOPE: bldg_class.startswith('F') — every F-subcode (F1 factory/heavy, F2/F4 warehouse, F5 light-mfg, F8 special/tank/utility-yard, F9 misc) is in scope; F8 explicitly INCLUDED. Self-storage E7 is an E-code, already excluded by the F filter. VALUE = curmkttot (99.9% filled on F, 3,245/3,248); tax bill = curtxbtot × class4_tax_rate — same mechanics as office/retail. SF = PLUTO BldgArea (100% filled, 3,248/3,248). RADIUS: same auto-ladder as retail (start 0.5, step 0.1) but a HIGHER ceiling — cap 1.75 mi (tunable 1.5-2.0). ~97% of F-codes fill within 1.0 mi (dense clusters); the raised cap serves the sparse ~3% tail (isolated / Staten Island / Manhattan). REFUSE beyond the cap; never reach further. SUBCODE MATCHING: subcode_match_first — prefer same F-subcode; pool to all-F ONLY when same-subcode can't reach min_comp_count, and DISCLOSE ('extended to all industrial subcodes to reach comparable parcels'). SF BAND: ±0.75 of subject BldgArea, proportional; size-dissimilar comps flagged loudly (retail ✕ marker + per-SF handling ported). RELAXATION ORDER (each step disclosed): same-subcode in-borough → same-subcode cross-borough → all-F cross-borough → refuse; GEOGRAPHY relaxes BEFORE subcode (use-type fidelity > proximity). MANHATTAN CASCADE (~30 F-codes citywide): in-borough same-subcode ±75% → if <8, reach NEAREST outer-borough cluster first → expand citywide only if that can't fill 8 → refuse if still <8; every cross-borough reach disclosed. BIG-BOX TAIL (BldgArea >= big_box_sf_threshold = 100,000 SF, ~67 parcels): drop the SF-band ceiling, take nearest-by-size CITYWIDE with NO distance cap (big_box_citywide_no_cap, retail K8 pattern); MANDATORY 'few true peers — directional, not precise' disclosure on every big-box screen; disclose max comp distance; loud size-dissimilar flags. 100K threshold is measurement-backed (peers collapse to 0-5 even at ±100%/3mi above 100K). Big-box is INDEPENDENT of the coverage disclosure (NYC big-box is high-coverage, median 2.0, only 2 of 67 below 0.30 — the coverage flag naturally never co-fires; no special logic). COVERAGE / LAND-VALUE. TWO complementary keys, deliberately 0.30 both now, tuned independently later. (1) coverage_exclusion_threshold (COMP-side): any comp with BldgArea/LotArea < it is land-dominant and is EXCLUDED from the per-SF calc only (stays in the value distribution + comp table, marked); disclosure 'N comp(s) excluded from per-SF as land-dominant'. (2) coverage_ratio_threshold (SUBJECT-side): when the SUBJECT's own coverage < it, the subject's per-SF is caveated (display-only). Net: subject land-dominant = per-SF caveated; comps land-dominant = excluded from per-SF. Legacy note: coverage_ratio_threshold fires when the SUBJECT has BldgArea/LotArea < 0.30 (land-dominant, per-building-SF skewed). Disclosure ONLY — non-computational, shows BldgArea/LotArea + LotArea for context; never filters/refuses. Threshold NYC-MEASURED from all 3,248 F-codes (median coverage 1.00, p25-p75 0.89-1.31, land-dominant tail unambiguous below 0.30, fires on 116 parcels = 3.6%); national industrial ratios (0.10-0.35) are meaningless for NYC and were NOT borrowed. F8 (median coverage 0.08) fires this on ~94% of F8 — CORRECT and intended (F8 is genuinely land-dominant, structure incidental); F8 stays in scope. MIN COMP COUNT: 8, refuse below (same as retail).
_condo_doc: Condo unit lots are excluded from the comp set: their gross_sqft provenance is unreliable for $/SF (whole-building-envelope error, see DECISIONS derived-tier rejection). A parcel is a condo unit lot if its building class starts with 'R' OR its lot number (BBL digits 7-10) >= condo_unit_lot_min. NOTE: not fully excluded upstream — many re-enter SF-eligibility via the roll gross_sqft fallback — so they are excluded here at the comp layer.
_exclude_non_positive_market_value_doc: Parcels with curmkttot <= 0 are tax-EXEMPT (no positive market value) and are NOT assessment peers — same categorical logic as the condo exclusion (DECISIONS 2026-06-20). Excluded from the comp universe entirely and routed to the exclusions table with reason NON_POSITIVE_MARKET_VALUE. A SUBJECT with curmkttot <= 0 gets a no-comparison refusal, not a distribution.
_min_comp_count_doc: Below this within the 1-mile cap, the screen REFUSES (DECISIONS LOCKED = 8). Tunable, logged for Phase 6.
_low_exact_caution_doc: When a SUCCESSFUL comp set has fewer than this many exact-class comps, attach a visible caution ('comp set is largely adjacent-class, interpret accordingly'). Warn, never refuse (DECISIONS 2026-06-20). Tunable, logged for Phase 6.
_metro_name_doc: Display name for this jurisdiction's metro, used in the expense-ratio benchmark note. NYC plugin renders 'New York City'.
_product_type_labels_doc: Building-class first letter -> product-type word for display (e.g. the expense-ratio note). v1 activates office only; others are mapped ahead of activation.
_expense_ratio_benchmarks_doc: Per-(metro, product_type) rule-of-thumb range for the real-estate-tax share of operating expenses, as [LOW, HIGH] percents. The note renders ONLY when a range exists for the subject's (metro, product_type). Populated for NYC: office = 40-50, retail = 35-45. The retail range mirrors office exactly (user-input-only, same 'general rule of thumb, not a sourced benchmark' label, no verdict framing); only the range and the word 'retail' differ. Add more (metro, product_type) entries later. NOT a sourced benchmark.
_class4_tax_rate_doc: LATEST-ADOPTED class-4 tax rate (FY2026 = 10.848%), applied to the FY2027 transitional taxable value because DOF has not yet published the FY2027 class-4 rate. Single source of truth: multiplies curtxbtot (transitional taxable) into the tax-bill SIGNAL (CONTEXT layer). MAINTENANCE: when DOF publishes the FY2027 class-4 rate, update THIS value only (one-line change); see DATA_SOURCES.md / DECISIONS.md.
